blocks.content: Shirone looked at Rian's broad back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian?
His back, as wide as a shield, always brought a sense of relief, but his temperament was different from before.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Shirone, I'll handle this.
There was no room for complaints with Rian, but one thing had to be clarified.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Be careful. This guy has Ymir's Demon.
Rian wanted to eliminate all potential dangers that could reach Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ymir?
In that sense, Ymir was a mountain that had to be overcome and the strongest rival.
Oink
Exchanging glances with Monoras over the great sword, Rian frowned.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That's hard to believe.
He was certainly strong, but his temperament was too low to be called Ymir.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's a complicated issue. Probably.
If Shirone postponed the explanation, Rian didn't intend to delve deeply either.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Got it. Leave this to me.
Rian also knew that subduing the president of the Fire Management Company was the top priority.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please.
As Shirone flew towards the city, Monoras twisted his body, ready to follow.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: I can't let him get away.
Rian called out.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Your opponent is me. The moment you turn your back, your body will be split in two, so keep that in mind.
Normally, Monoras would snort, but this time he couldn't move.
Rian's muscles twisted grotesquely, taking on the form of a complete Yaksha.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Quite…!
Before he could finish speaking, Rian's fist landed squarely on Monoras's jaw.
The pig's face seemed to be crushed as he flew hundreds of meters forward and crashed into the ground.
Boom!
As Rian pulled the great sword and stepped forward, Monoras burst through the smoke.
Oink!
Boom!
The moment the fist and the great sword collided again, a flash exploded, and a shockwave swept through.
Both were shocked by the resistance beyond imagination, and Monoras grinned.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: It's been a while since I had to use my strength.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: Why is pain so enjoyable?
Oink!
As the determined punch hit the great sword, the shock was transmitted through the handle.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: Ugh!
Rian's skin was torn to shreds, and Monoras's fist broke with a cracking sound.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: It still doesn't break?

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: cannot be destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: Whew.
As Rian slowly retreated and let go of the great sword, it fell to the ground with a clang.
Monoras, while looking at the great sword, also watched the chain connected to his palm.
What is that? Oink.
Gehenna.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's the fire that burns everything.
Rian, who had heated the great sword red, pulled the chain and aimed it.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Your demon… I will take it.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: If it was truly Ymir's Demon, Rian was unsure if he could completely purify it.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hmm.
Even Monoras, who had no interest in the system of hell, knew the chain of Gehenna.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: Hehe. Hehehe.
Monoras's muscles bulged, and for the first time, his eyes flashed with murderous intent.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: It was unmistakably Ymir's energy.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: I will consume.
Leading an energy greater than a mountain, Monoras lunged forward with his upper body.

blocks.speaker: Monoras
blocks.content: 'I will devour everything.'
Leaving Shirone to Monoras, Lete arrived at Marguerite's underground laboratory.

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: Boss! Please show mercy!
The generals all knelt and pleaded, but Lete's response was cold.

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: Marguerite, you have disappointed me.
Marguerite, who was stuck to the wall of the laboratory, was severely injured by hellfire.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: I won't make excuses. But I did it for Satan. You know that, don't you?

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: Satan?
Lete sneered.

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: Satan is just a creature born from the system of hell. It's not a world created for you to manipulate at will. The rules of the Fire Management Company are strict.
Marguerite nodded.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: I see. Now I understand. From the beginning… it had nothing to do with you how this world turned.

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: What do you want to say?
It wasn't a question to ask before death, so the generals looked puzzled.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: I just want to ask one thing. We've been together for 500 million years, so you'll answer, right? Or is that time just a moment to you?
The generals looked puzzled.

blocks.speaker: General
blocks.content: Grand Duke, what do you mean…
Marguerite asked as if blocking out the noise.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Why do administrators exist? Didn't it actually not matter what happened in this world? Whether Satan wins or Yahweh wins, whoever wins…
Marguerite's gaze pierced through Lete.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: As long as this world can be maintained, it doesn't matter to you, does it?
Lete had a sad expression.

blocks.speaker: Lete
blocks.content: You've lived too long, Marguerite.
What does it matter? You are just my children. If you hadn't betrayed me, this wouldn't have happened.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: Betrayal? What betrayal?
Marguerite frowned.

blocks.speaker: Marguerite
blocks.content: The most frightening thing is… The administrator's mission.
The integration of the system. When the systems you manage separately are integrated, we can go to the outside world. That's why you tried to prevent my alliance with Yahweh, isn't it?
